Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 072 [Sakikku 19][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[...] x x

(o i 1') [...]

o i 2'2'

[...] ana ŠA₃ GEŠTU-[MIN-šu₂ GARan-ma ḪUL] UGU-šu₂ [ZI]

(o i 2') [... you put] inside [his] ears [and the evil] afflicting his head [will be eradicated].

o i 3'3'

[* NA ina] ⸢E⸣.SIR₂ ina DU-šu₂ ana IGI-šu₂ ⸢ŠUB⸣ut IGI-MIN-šu₂ pal-⸢ka⸣

(o i 3') [(If) a man while] walking down the street tumbles head first, his eyes open wide, he does not know how to turn (them) (and) he cannot move his hands and feet of his own volition:

o i 4'4'

⸢tur-ra⸣ la₃ TUKU ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ⸢NI₂⸣-šu₂ la u₂-na-aš₂

o i 5'5'

NA BI ḪUL DABsu GIM AN.TA.ŠUB.BA ir-te-ne₂-ḫi-šu₂

(o i 5') an Evil One has seized that man, as if the falling sickness were continually afflicting him.

o i 6'6'

ŠURIM.d!še-riš NAGA.SI ḪAD₂.DU SUD₂ KI!?(AŠ.KU) GA!?(GIŠ.BUR₂)

(o i 6') You dry (and) crush ox-dung and glasswort. You mix it with the milk of a tabooed woman who has given birth to a son.

o i 7'7'

⸢MUNUS⸣::U₂.:ZUG₂:ti ša₂ ma-[ra] U₃.TU ḪI.ḪI

o i 8'8'

EN₂ ana ŠA₃ ŠID⸢nu ŠEŠ₂⸣-MEŠ-su AN.BAR ḫu-luḫ-ḫa

(o i 8') You recite the (appropriate) incantation. You rub him repeatedly, put (a bead of) smelted iron (or: iron (and) huluhhu-slag) around his neck and the Evil afflicting his head will be eradicated.

o i 9'9'

ina ⸢GU₂⸣-šu₂ GAR-ma ḪUL UGU-šu₂ ZIaḫ

o i 10'10'

* NA si-mat IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ KUR₂.KUR₂ir IGI-MIN-šu₂ it-ta-nap-ra-ra

(o i 10') (If) a man's facial countenance constantly changes, his eyes keep rolling around, he scratches his lips (and) chin and blood is dripping from his mouth (and) cannot be kept in: an Evil One has seized that man.

o i 11'11'

NUNDUN-šu₂ zu-qat-su u₂-lap-pat u UŠ₂ ina KA-šu₂ DUku

o i 12'12'

la ⸢i⸣-kal-lu-u NA BI ⸢ḪUL⸣ DABsu

o i 13'13'

u₂an-ki-nu-te u₂LAL SUD₂

(o i 13') You crush ankinūtu-plant and ašqulālu-plant, mix it with a dove's blood. You rub it on him and he will recover.

o i 14'14'

⸢KI UŠ₂ TU!?⸣mušen ḪI.ḪI ⸢ŠEŠ₂⸣-su-ma TIuṭ

o i 15'15'

[* NA ina GU₂]-šu₂ SIG₃iṣ-ma e-li-a-at

(o i 15') [(If) a man], having been struck [on] his [neck], has painful eyelids, is dripping pus (or): blood from his mouth, his hands and feet hang down slack (and) he cannot move his hands and feet: an Evil One has seized that man.

o i 16'16'

IGI-MIN-šu₂ ⸢GU₇-MEŠ⸣-šu₂ LUGUD : UŠ₂ ina KA-šu₂ ŠUB.ŠUB

o i 17'17'

ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN-⸢šu₂⸣ uš-⸢qa₂⸣-lal-⸢la⸣

o i 18'18'

ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ la u₂-na-aš₂

o i 19'19'

NA BI ḪUL ⸢DAB⸣[su] ana KAR-šu₂

(o i 19') In order to release him, you dry and crush sulphur and a tortoise's penis. You mix it with magnetite blood.1 You smear it over all his skin.

o i 20'20'

PIŠ₁₀.dID₂ GIŠ₃ [BAL].GIku₆

o i 21'21'

ḪAD₂.DU SUD₂ KI UŠ₂ ⸢na₄KA.GI⸣.NA DAB

o i 22'22'

ḪI.ḪI UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ DU₃-A.BI-šu₂-nu TAG.TAG

o i 23'23'

SAG!(BA.BAR) UGAmušen GE₆ TIqe₂

(o i 23') You take a black crow’s head. You wrap it in the hide (and) tendons of a dormouse. You put it around his neck and he will recover.

o i 24'24'

ina KUŠ SA PEŠ₂.gišUR₃.RA

o i 25'25'

GAG.GAG ina GU₂-šu₂ GAR-ma TI

o i 26'26'

* NA GIM AN.TA.ŠUB.BA ir-te-ne₂-ḫi-šu₂

(o i 26') If something like the falling sickness repeatedly afflicts a man

(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

la ⸢i⸣-[maḫ-ḫar ...]

(o ii 1') fails to [digest ...]

o ii 2'2'

ša₂-⸢ne₂-e⸣ [...]

(o ii 2') deputy [...]

o ii 3'3'

I₃.UDU UR.[GI₇ ...]

(o ii 3') Dog's fat [...].

o ii 4'4'

* MIN-ma a!(ZA-)⸢di! i⸣-[de-ek-ku-šu la₃ ZIbe₂]

(o ii 4') (If) ditto and [he will not get up] until they [make him do so (and) his belly] inces[santly] craves bread and beer:

o ii 5'5'

NINDA u KAŠ [ŠA₃-šu₂] URU₄.[URU₄iš ŠU DINGIR ga-aš₂-ri]

(o ii 5') [Hand of the valiant deity], the deputy power of [Nergal].

o ii 6'6'

d⸢ALAD⸣ ša₂-ne₂-e ⸢d⸣[NERGALₓ(U.GUR) ana KAR-šu₂ SAG.DU]

(o ii 6') [In order to release him, you put] a raven's [head] around his neck. [You repeatedly rub him with sikillu-plant] and tamarisk (processed) in oil [and he will recover].

o ii 7'7'

BURU₅!(ŠIR)mušen ina GU₂-šu₂ [GARan u₂SIKIL]

o ii 8'8'

gišŠINIG ina I₃+GIŠ ⸢ŠEŠ₂⸣-[MEŠ-su-ma TI]

o ii 9'9'

* MIN-ma uš-tan-na-aḫ [E₂.GAR₈]

(o ii 9') (If) ditto and he lies in agony, [he ... the wall] with his hands (and) feet, he [cannot sleep] in daytime or at night:

o ii 10'10'

ina ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ i-[...]

o ii 11'11'

ur-ra u GE₆ la ⸢i⸣-[ṣal-lal ŠU DINGIR šam-ri]

(o ii 11') [Hand of the ferocious deity], deputy power of [...].

o ii 12'12'

dALAD ša₂-ne₂-⸢e⸣ [d...]

o ii 13'13'

* MIN-ma IGI-MEŠ-[šu₂ ...]

(o ii 13') (If) ditto and his face [...], [he is unable] to talk [..., his ears] are buzzing, [...]:

o ii 14'14'

da-ba-ba [la₃ ZU ... GEŠTU?-MIN?-šu₂]

o ii 15'15'

i-šag-⸢gu?⸣-[ma? ...]

o ii 16'16'

ŠU DINGIR [... dALAD]

(o ii 16') Hand of the [...] deity, deputy [power of ...].

o ii 17'17'

ša₂-ne₂-⸢e⸣ [d...]

o ii 18'18'

ana ina ⸢ŠU⸣ [DINGIR ... KAR-šu₂]

(o ii 18') In order to [release him] from the Hand [of the ... deity, you ...] huluhhu-slag [...] ... [...].

o ii 19'19'

ḫu-⸢luḫ⸣-[ḫa ...]

x [...]

⸢da⸣-[ba-ba la₃ ZU ...]

r ii 7'7'

ŠU DINGIR [... dALAD ša₂-ne₂-e]

(r ii 7') Hand of the [...] deity, [deputy power] of the god ...[...].

r ii 8'8'

dx [... ana ina ŠU DINGIR ... KAR-šu₂]

(r ii 8') [In order to release him from the Hand of the ... deity] ... [...], stone [...], [you string] on a [linen] thread. [...] and ... [...] of wool, his feet [...].

na₄[...]

* MIN-ma uš!?-[tan-na-aḫ? ...]

(r ii 14') (If) ditto and he lies in agony [...], all night long he cannot [sleep ...], when drinking water [...] his face [...]:

r ii 15'15'

⸢kal⸣ GE₆ ⸢la⸣ [i-ṣal-lal ...]

r ii 16'16'

A ina NAG [...]

r ii 17'17'

IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ [... ŠU DINGIR ... dALAD]

⸢ša₂-ne₂⸣-[e d...]

ša₂ [...]

r ii 22'22'

ina ⸢GU₂⸣-[šu₂ GARan-ma TI]

Column iii
r iii 1r iii 1

* GIG-ma KA-šu₂ BAD.BADte ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂

(r iii 1) (If) a man in his illness constantly opens his mouth and he loses all the strength in his hands and feet:

r iii 22

i-⸢par-ru⸣-ra ŠU DINGIR mu-⸢un⸣-<ni>-ši dALAD

(r iii 2) Hand of the enfeebling deity, deputy power of Anu.

r iii 33

ša₂-ne₂-e da-nu₃

r iii 44

ana ina ŠU DINGIR mu-un-ni-ši KAR-[šu₂]

(r iii 4) In order to release him from the Hand of the enfeebling deity, you crush together a she-lamb's ..., a deer's horn and ankinūtu-plant. You rub him nine times if he is male, seven times if she is female and (s)he will recover.

r iii 55

PA pu₂-ḫat-ti SI DARA₃.MAŠ1

r iii 66

u₂an-ki-nu-te 1niš SUD₂

r iii 77

šum-ma NITA 9-šu₂ šum-ma MUNUS 7-šu₂

r iii 88

ŠEŠ₂-MEŠ-su-ma TI

r iii 99

* GIG-ma KUM₂ ŠUB.[ŠUB-su]-ma u₂-ta-ṣal u2

(r iii 9) If in his illness he experiences [recurrent] attacks of fever, he becomes numb and also has no sa[li]va:2

r iii 1010

⸢ru-ti la₃ TUKU ŠU⸣ dza-qi₂-qi₂ dALAD

(r iii 10) Hand of Zaqiqu, the deputy power of the god [...].3

r iii 1111

ša₂-ne₂-⸢e⸣ d[...] ana ina ŠU dza-qi₂-qi₂ KAR-šu₂

(r iii 11) In order to release him from the Hand of Zaqiqu, you put ... and algae, (and) male and female iron around his neck. Finally, you repeatedly rub him with fox-grape and he will recover.

r iii 1212

x x x-na KA A.AB.BA AN.BAR NITA u MUNUS

r iii 1313

⸢ina GU₂⸣-šu₂ GAR u gišGEŠTIN KA₅.A ŠEŠ₂-MEŠ-su-ma TI

r iii 1414

* MIN-ma IR la-ba-ṣi ma-at-tam TUKU.TUKUši

(r iii 14) (If) ditto and he excessively transpires the sweat caused by the labāṣu-demon and in addition he experiences recurrent attacks of feverish shivering:

r iii 1515

u ḫur-ba-šu ŠUB.ŠUB-su ŠU DINGIR aš-ṭi3

(r iii 15) Hand of the stiff deity, deputy power of Ea.

r iii 1616

dALAD ša₂-ne₂-e de₂-a ana ina ŠU DINGIR aš-ṭi ⸢KAR⸣-šu₂

(r iii 16) In order to release him from the Hand of the stiff deity, you dry with fire a chameleon's fat and a dalīlu-frog that lives amidst the pebbles.

r iii 1717

I₃.UDU a-a-ar DINGIR da-⸢li⸣-la ša₂ ŠA₃ na₄PEŠ₄

r iii 1818

tur-ar₂ gišŠINIG ESIR.U₄.A PIŠ₁₀.dID₂

(r iii 18) You put (them along with) tamarisk, asphalt, black sulphur, yellow sulphur, and mēsallu-salt around his neck; finally, you repeatedly rub him with amhara-plant and he will recover.

r iii 1919

⸢ru⸣-ti-dID₂ MUN eme-sal-⸢li₃⸣ ina GU₂-šu₂ GAR

r iii 2020

u u₂am-ḫa-ra ŠEŠ₂-MEŠ-su-ma TI

r iii 2121

* MIN-ma I₃+GIŠ ŠEŠ₂-MEŠ-[su]-⸢ma⸣ ina SU-šu₂ la₃ TAG₄!

(r iii 21) (If) ditto and despite your repeatedly rubbing [him] with oil, it (= the disease) does not leave his body, [thereafter], however, he lavishly spends on oil and then he stops transpiring the sweat and it does leave him:

r iii 2222

⸢EGIR?-šu₂?⸣ I₃+GIŠ u₂-šar-⸢raḫ IR?⸣ la₃ TUKU-ma TAG₄!-⸢šu⸣

r iii 2323

[dALAD ša₂-ne₂]-⸢e DINGIR⸣.MAḪ

(r iii 23) [Deputy power] of Belet-ili.

r iii 2424

[... I₃].⸢UDU? ANŠE!?⸣.KUR.RA I₃.UDU

(r iii 24) [...] horse fat, the fat of [...]. He shall wash his hands and he will recover.

r iii 2525

[...] ŠU-MIN!?-šu₂!? ⸢LUḪ?-ma?⸣ TI

r iii 2626

[KI.MIN? ...-ma] TI

(r iii 26') [Alternatively ... and] he will recover;

r iii 2727

[KI.MIN? ...-ma] TI

(r iii 27') [Alternatively ... and] he will recover.

r iii 2828

[...]-MIN-šu₂

(r iii 28') [...] his [...]s

(rest of column missing)

1Kinnier Wilson takes PA = SÌG, for SÍG = šipāt-; however, 'wool' hardly qualifies for being the object of sâku. Could SÌG here possibly represent a form of šahāṭu 'to flay'? or: PA = kappu? (paw?)

2Scurlock and Andersen (§19.371) read instead: DIŠ GIG-ma IZI.ŠUB.[BA IGI]-ma u₂-ta-ni-u BABBAR-ti NU TUKU# ŠU# ...

3Stol, Epilepsy, p.6, n.19: {d}AŠ.DI = {d}AŠ.DU = diʾu or šuruppû.

1 This will denote the reddish fluid which is secreted by iron ore when it has been brought into contact with water , a substance , therefore , comparable to bog ore or limonite.

2 Scurlock and Andersen (§19.371 ) give a very different interpretation : "If (he ) is sick and [he sees ] a lightning bolt and it (looks ) altered (and ) has no whiteness,"

3 The context is compatible with Šamaš—as the god of mantic arts and revelation , whose nocturnal avatar is the dream—as well as Ellil.# Scurlock - Andersen argue that it's Enlil.

Created by Henry Stadhouders for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2012 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P363487
